bikes.motor: 750W (1130W peak) geared rear hub, 80Nm, torque sensor
bikes.year_notes: Launched Jan 17, 2023 with the torque sensor and BC 280 color display from day one (Aventon announcement blog; Electrek 1/17/2023; EBR review). The founder-suspected running changes (torque-sensor timing, display revision) happened BETWEEN generations, not within the .2 run: Aventure gen 1 (2021-2022) had a cadence sensor and older display and is a separate model, as is Aventure 3 (Feb 2025: gravity-cast frame, Shimano Altus 8-speed, 1440W peak boost, ACU 4G/GPS, Apple Watch). No verified mid-production component change within Aventure.2 2023-2025, so no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Aventon's best-selling fat-tire all-rounder. Huge install base means steady takeoff supply: knobby tires, wedge saddles, and Zoom forks come off constantly.

stock_parts.comp_note: eBay Zoom fork listings run $24-95 used but none are confirmed Aventure-spec solds. Priced low; unbranded OEM forks move slowly. Same fork across the 2023-2025 .2 run; no revision evidenced, no year split.

upgrade.name: USD-8 fat bike air fork
upgrade.note: The stock Zoom is a pogo stick with a lockout. The USD-8 actually damps instead of just bouncing.

stock_parts.comp_note: New from Aventon ~$550; generic 48V 15Ah packs sell new on eBay for $170-265, which caps what buyers pay for a used proprietary pack. One eBay sold confirmed but price hidden. Priced low. Same 720Wh pack carried over from gen 1 through the whole .2 run (Ebike Escape); no capacity or connector change evidenced, no year split.

upgrade.name: Aventure.2 spare battery 720Wh
upgrade.note: Proprietary mount, so a second OEM pack is the only real play. Double your range, keep the warranty.

stock_parts.comp_note: New retail $69.99 direct from Aventon. Two eBay used listings found (Pace 500 and Sinch 2 fitment) but no confirmed sold prices. Buyers are owners with cracked screens. BC 280 was fitted from the Jan 2023 launch; no display revision evidenced within the .2 run (revisions were generational: gen 1 monochrome LCD, Aventure 3 ACU-era display), so no year split.

New Kenda 26x4 equivalents (Juggernaut, Krusade) list $30-55 per tire on eBay, so a used unbranded takeoff pair tops out around $35-45. Street-tire conversions make this the most common Aventure takeoff.

upgrade.name: Speedster 26x4.0 street tires (pair)
upgrade.note: Knobbies hum like a truck on pavement. Speedsters cut the noise and add real range.

stock_parts.comp_note: No confirmed solds; generic OEM saddles sell $10-20 on eBay. First thing most owners swap per forum threads.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select saddle
upgrade.note: The stock wedge punishes anyone over an hour in the seat. The Cloud-9 is the default fix on r/aventon.

stock_parts.comp_note: Near-zero resale demand for stock rubber grips. Priced at parts-bin level.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Numb hands on long rides come from round grips. GP1 wings fix it for forty bucks.

stock_parts.comp_note: Owner reports of failing bearings in stock pedals. Used OEM platforms sell $8-15 when they sell at all.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Stock pedal bearings die young. Chesters grip wet shoes and shrug off rock strikes.

stock_parts.stock_part: Aventon hydraulic disc brake set, 180mm rotors (levers + calipers)
stock_parts.comp_note: Forum complaints about rotor harmonics and thin 1.8mm stock rotors drive upgrades. No confirmed used-set solds; new e-bike hydraulic sets run $85-160, capping the takeoff. Hydraulic across the whole .2 run; no brake revision evidenced, no year split.

upgrade.name: 4-piston hydraulic brake set with 203mm rotors
upgrade.note: A 77lb bike at 28mph deserves 4-piston calipers and thicker rotors. Stock 2-pistons fade on long descents.

stock_parts.stock_part: Aventon integrated rear rack (stock on Aventure.2)
stock_parts.comp_note: Rack became included-in-base-price at the .2 launch (Ebike Escape: gen-1 sold it separately), so every 2023-2025 bike has one. Demand is thin since every bike ships with it. Priced accordingly.

bikes.motor: 500W (900W peak) geared rear hub, torque sensor
bikes.year_notes: Launched Aug 2022 as Aventon's first torque-sensor bike (Aventon announcement). Zoom Aria 65mm coil fork, Tektro HD-E350 hydraulics with 180mm rotors, Shimano 8-speed, and the full-color display all confirmed at launch (EBR, InsideEVs, Ebike Escape). Still sold new at $999 into 2026 (Feb 2026 reviews) after the Level 3 (early 2025: 36V system, 733Wh, ACU) arrived as a separate model, so v1's 2022-2025 extended to 2022-2026. No mid-production component change evidenced across the run; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Torque-sensor commuter workhorse. Ships loaded with rack, fenders, Zoom Aria fork, and Tektro hydros, so takeoffs skew toward comfort parts.

stock_parts.stock_part: Zoom Aria suspension fork, 65mm travel, with lockout and preload
stock_parts.comp_note: Travel corrected to 65mm coil per EBR's Level.2 review. Zoom forks list $24-95 used on eBay with no confirmed Aria solds. Commuters rarely swap it, so supply and demand are both thin. Same fork 2022-2026; no year split.

Facebook Marketplace listing referenced $450 new retail for the Level pack. Generic 48V packs at $170-265 new cap the used ceiling. Priced low. 672Wh spec constant across the run (Level 3's 733Wh pack is a different 36V system on a separate model); no year split.

upgrade.name: Level.2 spare battery 672Wh
upgrade.note: Commuters kill packs with daily charging. A second OEM battery doubles range and halves cycle wear.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same BC280 unit across the Aventon lineup. New retail $69.99; used asks $40-55, no confirmed solds. The color display was the Level.2's launch upgrade over the gen-1 Level's monochrome LCD (EBR) and stayed constant within the .2 run; no year split.
27.5x2.1 confirmed by EBR. No resale datapoints found for the OEM tire specifically. Unbranded takeoff commuter pairs move at $20-30. Priced low.

upgrade.name: Marathon Plus 27.5x2.15 tires (pair)
upgrade.note: A commuter with a flat is a paperweight. Marathon Plus is the boring correct answer.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same wedge saddle as the Aventure. Swapped early and often; resale demand minimal.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select saddle
upgrade.note: Upright commuter posture puts all your weight on the seat. Get one that accepts the job.

stock_parts.comp_note: Parts-bin value only.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Same fix as every commuter: flat wings, no more pins and needles.

stock_parts.comp_note: Cheap OEM platforms; near-zero resale.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Cheapest contact-point upgrade on the bike and the one you feel every pedal stroke.

stock_parts.comp_note: Suspension seatpost is THE Level.2 comfort mod, so rigid takeoff posts pile up. Almost no buyers; priced at floor.

upgrade.name: NCX SP12 suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: No rear suspension means your spine is the shock. The NCX does the job for under a hundred bucks.

stock_parts.stock_part: Tektro HD-E350 hydraulic disc brake set, 180mm rotors
stock_parts.comp_note: Model pinned down in v2: Ebike Escape and EBR both identify Tektro HD-E350 hydros with 180mm rotors, constant across the run. New HD-E350 sets run $85-160, so used takeoff sets land near $45. Takeoffs mostly come from crash repairs. No year split.

bikes.year_notes: Third-generation Pace launched early 2023 with the torque sensor, seat-stay turn signals, and BC 280 color display from day one (EBR and Ebike Escape reviews; 99 Spokes lists it as a 2023 model). Replaced by the Pace 4 (announced April 17, 2025: 36V system shared with Level 3/Abound siblings, ACU smart tech, step-thru only) which is a separate model. No mid-production component change evidenced within the 500.3 run; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Comfort cruiser with torque sensor and turn signals. Rigid fork and relaxed geometry; takeoffs concentrate in contact points and the BC280 display.

stock_parts.comp_note: Smaller pack than Aventure/Level so priced below them. No confirmed solds; generic new packs cap the ceiling. Priced low. 614Wh spec constant 2023-2025 (Pace 4 moved to 36V on a separate model); no year split.

upgrade.name: Pace 500.3 spare battery 614Wh
upgrade.note: Only OEM packs fit the frame slot. Second battery beats range anxiety.

stock_parts.comp_note: eBay listing found specifically for a Pace 500 BC280 takeoff; retailer fitment lists (Bicycle Warehouse, Hamilton Electric Bikes) confirm BC 280 on the Pace 500.3. New retail $69.99; no confirmed sold price. Priced under used asks. Fitted from launch; no year split.

stock_parts.stock_part: Aventon rounded cruiser saddle (Pace-specific, thicker than standard wedge)
stock_parts.comp_note: EBR confirms the Pace gets a thicker rounded seat, not the wedge. Slightly better than wedge resale but still a saddle.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select saddle
upgrade.note: The stock seat is decent for a cruiser. Riders past 15 miles a day still want the big spring.

stock_parts.comp_note: EBR confirms ergonomic lock-on grips stock. Parts-bin resale value.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Swept bars plus GP1 wings is peak comfort cruiser cockpit.

stock_parts.comp_note: Street tread already, so fewer swaps than the fat bikes. No solds found; priced with the Level pair.

upgrade.name: Marathon Plus 27.5 tires (pair)
upgrade.note: Flat protection is the only tire upgrade a cruiser needs.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same OEM platforms across the lineup.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Bigger platform, real grip, bearings that last.

stock_parts.comp_note: Rigid fork plus rigid post makes the suspension seatpost the go-to Pace comfort mod. Takeoff posts are near-worthless.

upgrade.name: NCX SP12 suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: On a fully rigid cruiser this is the single biggest comfort gain per dollar.

bikes.year_notes: Launched Aug 2023 (Electrek review 8/8/2023) with torque sensor, Tektro mechanical 180mm discs, 300-lumen headlight with turn-signal tail lights, and a full-color display from day one. Succeeded by the Soltera 2.5 -- a separate model, listed as 2024 by 99 Spokes and 2025 by other sources -- which moved to Tektro hydraulic brakes, Kenda 700x38c reflective tires, a double-butted frame with shorter reach, and head-tube cable routing. No mid-production change evidenced within the .2 itself; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: 46lb budget city bike with a torque sensor. Mechanical discs are the number-one owner complaint, making brake takeoffs the signature Soltera trade-in.

stock_parts.stock_part: Tektro mechanical disc brake set, 180mm rotors, 2-piston calipers (levers + cable calipers)
stock_parts.comp_note: Brand corrected in v2: reviews (ebicycles, Tom's Guide) identify the calipers as Tektro mechanical with 180mm rotors. catalog_name kept for DB merge. Mech-to-hydraulic conversion is the defining Soltera.2 upgrade, so takeoff supply is high and demand near zero. Floor pricing. Mechanical across the whole .2 run (hydraulics arrived with the separate Soltera 2.5); no year split.

upgrade.name: HD-E350 e-bike hydraulic disc brake set (front + rear)
upgrade.note: Cable discs on a 28mph-capable bike is a cost cut you fix first. Pre-bled set bolts right on.

stock_parts.comp_note: Small 36V pack worth well under the 48V units. No confirmed solds; priced low against generic 36V packs at $120-180 new. 36V 9.6Ah spec constant across the run (some reviews round to 360Wh; 36V x 9.6Ah = 345.6Wh); no year split.

upgrade.name: Soltera.2 spare battery 346Wh
upgrade.note: The 346Wh pack is the bike's weak spot. A spare in the pannier doubles a thin range.

stock_parts.comp_note: MERGED in v2: v1 kept this as a separate LCD entry pending confirmation. Retailer fitment listings now confirm the shared SKU (Hamilton Electric Bikes lists the BC 280 for 'Pace 500, Sinch, Soltera.2') and reviews describe the same app-connected full-color unit. Merged into the fleet-wide BC280 row; fitted from launch, no year split.
700x38c confirmed by reviews. No solds found for the OEM tire. Generic 700c takeoff pairs move at $15-25. (The reflective-sidewall Kenda 700x38c belongs to the separate Soltera 2.5.)

upgrade.name: Marathon 700x38 tires (pair)
upgrade.note: Commuters flat on glass and grates. Marathons end that conversation.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same wedge as the rest of the lineup. Minimal resale.

upgrade.name: Respiro Moderate saddle
upgrade.note: Sportier posture than the cruisers, so skip the big spring and get a proper foam saddle.

stock_parts.comp_note: Parts-bin value.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Cheap bike, long commutes. Hands notice first.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same OEM platforms as the rest of the lineup.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Light bike deserves pedals that do not rattle loose in a season.

bikes.year_notes: Launched early 2023 with a torque sensor from day one (Ebike Escape: 'cargo ebike with a torque sensor'; EBR) -- v1's motor line omitted the torque sensor, corrected here. Uses the shared BC 280 display (Aventon BC 280 retailer fitment includes Abound). Abound SR and Abound LR (2025: 36V systems, ACU smart module with 4G/GPS, electronic locking) are separate models, not year variants. No mid-production component change evidenced on the original Abound; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: 81lb longtail cargo hauler with a 143lb-rated rack and hydraulic dropper post. Takeoffs skew utilitarian; the 720Wh pack is the money part.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same 720Wh capacity as Aventure but a model-specific SKU, so kept separate from the Aventure pack. Priced to match; no confirmed solds. Constant across the 2023-2025 run (the 36V packs belong to the separate SR/LR); no year split.

upgrade.name: Abound spare battery 720Wh
upgrade.note: Cargo miles with kids aboard eat watt-hours. Second pack or shorter school runs, pick one.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared BC280 platform display; Abound fitment confirmed via Aventon's BC 280 compatibility (covers Abound line). Same $69.99 new anchor, no confirmed used solds. No year split.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock suspension fork, 50mm travel with lockout
stock_parts.comp_note: 50mm switchable-lockout fork confirmed by OutdoorGearLab/EBR. Cargo-specific 20-inch fork with almost no cross-compatibility. Thin market both directions.
No resale market found for stock 20-inch cargo takeoff tires. Nobody is buying these used.

stock_parts.stock_part: Aventon hydraulic dropper seatpost (stock on Abound)
stock_parts.comp_note: Droppers have a real used market but this is an OEM unit with unknown cross-fit. Priced conservatively.
Standard saddle economics: high supply, low demand.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select saddle
upgrade.note: Hauling kids means stop-and-go riding all day. Wide and sprung wins.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared OEM platform pedals.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Wet school-run mornings need pedals with real pins.

stock_parts.stock_part: Aventon hydraulic disc brake set, 180mm rotors
stock_parts.comp_note: Same hydraulic platform as the Aventure. Takeoffs come from riders upgrading for loaded stopping power. Hydraulic across the run; no year split.

upgrade.name: 4-piston hydraulic brake set with 203mm rotors
upgrade.note: 440lb gross weight on 2-piston brakes is optimistic. Four pistons and 203s are the honest spec.

bikes.year_notes: Launched Feb 2024 (Forbes 2/27/2024, GearJunkie first look). Full spec verified across EBR, OutdoorGearLab, Singletracks: RockShox 35 Silver TK 130mm, SRAM NX Eagle 12-speed, SRAM DB8 4-piston, Maxxis Rekon 29x2.4, 36V 708Wh, plus a stock dropper post (added as a row in v2 -- v1 missed it). The Ramblas ADV (2025) is a separate model: quieter A100 revision, tubeless-ready rims (original rims are NOT tubeless-ready per Ebike Escape), SRAM DB6 brakes (a step DOWN from the original's DB8), revised dropper, still no ACU. No mid-production change evidenced on the original; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Sub-$3k mid-drive eMTB with name-brand parts (RockShox, SRAM, Maxxis). The only Aventon whose takeoffs have a genuine enthusiast resale market.

stock_parts.stock_part: RockShox 35 Silver TK, 130mm travel, tapered, Boost
stock_parts.comp_note: eBay listings found at ~C$261 (120mm, used) and ~C$310 (160mm, new takeoff), roughly US$190-225 asks. Sold medians run below asks; priced at $150. Real market exists but under 3 confirmed solds.

upgrade.name: Pike Select 130mm fork
upgrade.note: The 35 Silver is a coil fork doing its best. A Pike transforms the front end on real trails.

stock_parts.stock_part: SRAM NX Eagle 12-speed (derailleur, shifter, cassette, crank)
stock_parts.comp_note: eBay NX Eagle groupsets list $125-559 new depending on completeness; a used 4-piece takeoff realistically clears ~$140. High-volume market but no confirmed sold prices in results.

upgrade.name: GX Eagle upgrade kit (derailleur + shifter)
upgrade.note: NX shifts fine until it is muddy. GX is the cheapest real improvement mid-drive torque deserves.

stock_parts.stock_part: SRAM DB8 4-piston hydraulic disc brake set
stock_parts.comp_note: DB8s retail ~$95 per wheel new. Used takeoff sets should clear $80. Already 4-piston, so takeoffs mostly come from lever-feel preference swaps. DB8 is original-Ramblas only -- the 2025 ADV downgraded to DB6 (separate model), which slightly boosts DB8 takeoff appeal. No year split within the original run.

upgrade.name: Code R brake set (front + rear)
upgrade.note: DB8s stop fine. Codes stop fine all the way down the mountain, every lap.

stock_parts.comp_note: Used takeoff Rekons routinely move at $25-35 per tire in classifieds. Priced a pair at $50; no confirmed sold triplet. Note: original Ramblas rims are not tubeless-ready (Ebike Escape ADV review), so takeoff tires here ran tubes.

upgrade.name: Minion DHF/DHR II 29x2.4 combo (pair)
upgrade.note: Rekons roll fast and wash out in corners. Minions bite. Standard eMTB swap.

stock_parts.comp_note: Newest and priciest Aventon pack but zero used-market history yet. Priced with the 48V packs to stay conservative. 708Wh spec constant across the 2024-2025 run; no year split.

upgrade.name: Ramblas spare battery 708Wh
upgrade.note: Turbo mode on trails drains it in 30 miles. Spare pack in the truck doubles the ride day.

stock_parts.comp_note: Row ADDED in v2: the Ramblas ships with a dropper post, confirmed indirectly by Ebike Escape's ADV review crediting the ADV with an 'updated dropper' with improved lever actuation versus the original's. Unbranded OEM unit; generic used droppers move at $40-80. No confirmed solds; priced low.
OEM saddle, personal-fit item, minimal resale.

upgrade.name: Volt saddle
upgrade.note: Saddles are personal but the Volt is the safest default in mountain biking.

stock_parts.comp_note: Parts-bin value.

upgrade.name: GE10 Evo grips
upgrade.note: Thin OEM rubber beats your palms on descents. Proper MTB grips are cheap insurance.

stock_parts.comp_note: Throw-in pedals on an otherwise name-brand build. First swap for most buyers.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Real pins, rebuildable, cheap. No reason to ride the stockers.

bikes.year_notes: Years corrected from v1's 2021-2024: announced late 2021 but shipped/reviewed spring 2022 (Electrek 5/2022, EBR, Electrified Reviews 2022). All dated base-model reviews confirm unbranded MECHANICAL 180mm disc brakes, 500W/65Nm hub, Shimano Tourney 7-speed, 48V 14.4Ah UL pack. Some comparison sites now list the Discover 1 with hydraulic brakes and a 3.5in display -- that spec traces to the Discover 1 Plus (2024: hydraulics, 3.5in backlit LCD, 28mph, 60lb, 15-lux headlight), a separately named model that replaced the base bike. No evidence of a mid-production brake or display change on the base Discover 1 itself, so no year splits. Discover 2 (Mar 2024) is also a separate model.
bikes.blurb: Comfort-first step-thru with a UL-listed 692Wh pack. Mechanical brakes and a flimsy fork (reviewers' words) define the upgrade path and the takeoff stream.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock mechanical disc brake set, 180mm rotors
stock_parts.comp_note: Mechanical confirmed by every dated 2022 base-model review (Electrek: '180mm mechanical disc brakes with motor cut-off'; EBR brake test). Hydraulic references online are the 2024 Discover 1 Plus, a separate model -- no year split. Mech discs on a 60lb ebike drive the hydraulic conversion, so takeoffs are plentiful and nearly unsellable. Floor pricing.

upgrade.name: HD-E350 e-bike hydraulic disc brake set (front + rear)
upgrade.note: The single most requested Discover 1 fix. Pre-bled kit, one afternoon, done.

stock_parts.comp_note: UL cert helps resale but no confirmed solds surfaced. Velotric sells spares new (price not confirmed in results); priced low against the generic-pack ceiling. 48V 14.4Ah spec constant across the 2022-2024 run; no year split.

upgrade.name: Velotric spare battery pack (Discover 1)
upgrade.note: Proprietary tray, so OEM spare is the only option. All battery sales final per Velotric.

stock_parts.comp_note: Velotric sells replacements direct, which caps the used price. Buyers are crash-damage owners. No solds found. The 3.5in backlit LCD belongs to the separate Discover 1 Plus (2024); base Discover 1 kept this unit throughout -- no year split.
Reviewers call it flimsy but there is no standard aftermarket swap for this fitment. Thin takeoff market.
Unbranded gumwalls have essentially no resale demand. Priced at floor.

upgrade.name: Big Ben 26x2.15 tires (pair)
upgrade.note: Unbranded gumwalls flat early and often. Big Bens keep the cruiser look and add puncture belts.

stock_parts.comp_note: Wide comfort saddle stock, so swap rate is lower than Aventon but still steady. Minimal resale.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select saddle
upgrade.note: Stock seat is decent. Bigger riders still land here.

stock_parts.comp_note: Parts-bin value.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Upright bars load your palms. GP1s spread the load.

stock_parts.comp_note: Generic OEM platforms.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Same story as every OEM pedal: fine until the bearings quit.

stock_parts.comp_note: Ships on every Discover, so used demand is weak. (The Discover 1 Plus dropped the standard rack per comparisons -- another marker separating Plus takeoffs from base-model ones.)

bikes.motor: 750W (1100W peak) geared rear hub, 75Nm, SensorSwap torque/cadence
bikes.year_notes: Launch verified as March 29, 2024 (PR Newswire announcement; Electrek review 3/29/2024) -- v1's 2024-2026 years CONFIRMED (verifier's 2023 hypothesis was wrong). 750W/75Nm motor, SensorSwap torque/cadence switching, 706Wh UL 2271/IPX7 pack, 3.5in color TFT, hydraulic brakes, and 8-speed drivetrain all present from launch. Discover 3 announced spring 2026 (Velotric PR 'Five New Ebikes for Spring 2026') as a separate successor. No mid-production component change evidenced; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: Feature-dense commuter: torque sensor, 3.5in color display, hydraulic brakes, full accessory kit. Well-specced stock, so takeoffs concentrate in contact points.

stock_parts.comp_note: Velotric confirms this SKU fits Discover 2, Summit 1, and Nomad 2, which widens the buyer pool. Still no used solds; priced low. Constant across the run; no year split.

upgrade.name: Velotric spare battery 705.6Wh
upgrade.note: Cross-compatible across three Velotric models. Best battery investment in their lineup.

stock_parts.stock_part: Velotric 3.5in color LCD display with cruise control and ride modes
stock_parts.comp_note: Velotric's replacement guide covers D2/S1/T1 ST Plus/N2/B1 with one display SKU, so takeoffs have a multi-model buyer pool. Factory replacements cap the price. No solds found. Fitted from the Mar 2024 launch; no year split.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock suspension fork, 80mm travel with lockout
stock_parts.comp_note: Adequate stock fork, rare swap, thin market.
27.5in wheels confirmed (Discover 2 moved up from the Discover 1's 26in per EBR). No OEM-specific solds found. Priced with other unbranded commuter pairs.

upgrade.name: Marathon Plus 27.5 tires (pair)
upgrade.note: Daily commuters buy flat protection once and stop thinking about it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Standard saddle economics.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select saddle
upgrade.note: Comfort commuter, comfort saddle. Simple.

stock_parts.comp_note: Parts-bin value.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Forty bucks against numb hands. Easy call.

stock_parts.comp_note: Generic OEM platforms.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: The upgrade everyone makes by year two anyway.

stock_parts.comp_note: Already hydraulic from the factory, so takeoffs come from crash repair, not upgrades. Thin supply, thin demand. No year split.

bikes.year_notes: 2022-2024 run verified (Electrified Reviews 2022; Electrek 3/27/2023). Verifier specifically checked the brake spec on suspicion of an early mechanical fitment: dated reviews from launch onward all confirm unbranded 2-piston HYDRAULIC 180mm discs (Electrek called the hydraulic choice a value standout) -- v1 is correct, no brake split. CST BFT 26x4 tires and the 691Wh Samsung/LG UL pack constant across the run. Nomad 1 Plus (2024: 3.5in display refresh) and Nomad 2 are separate models. No mid-production change evidenced; no year splits.
bikes.blurb: UL-listed fat-tire value bike. CST BFT knobbies come off for street tires constantly, and the shared display and battery SKUs help takeoff liquidity.

stock_parts.stock_part: CST BFT 26x4.0 fat tires with puncture liner (pair)
stock_parts.comp_note: CST BFTs are a real branded tire that sells new around $40-55 each, so takeoff pairs clear ~$40. Street conversion is the top Nomad mod. No confirmed sold triplet.

upgrade.name: Speedster 26x4.0 street tires (pair)
upgrade.note: If the Nomad never leaves pavement, the knobbies are just noise and drag. Speedsters fix both.

stock_parts.comp_note: Samsung/LG 21700 cells and UL cert support resale, but no confirmed solds surfaced. Priced low with the Discover 1 pack. Same pack spec on the Nomad 1 Plus, so buyer pool spans both; no year split within Nomad 1.

upgrade.name: Velotric spare battery pack (Nomad 1)
upgrade.note: Fat tires eat charge. Spare pack or shorter rides.

stock_parts.comp_note: Unbranded OEM fat fork; upgraders pull it but few buyers want it. Priced low.

upgrade.name: USD-8 fat bike air fork
upgrade.note: Same swap as every fat hub-drive: air spring and real damping over the stock boinger.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared SKU with Discover 1 widens the buyer pool slightly. Factory replacements cap the price. The 3.5in display belongs to the separate Nomad 1 Plus/Nomad 2; base Nomad 1 kept this unit throughout -- no year split.

stock_parts.stock_part: Unbranded 2-piston hydraulic disc brake set, 180mm rotors
stock_parts.comp_note: Hydraulic from launch per dated 2022-2023 reviews (Electrek, Ebike Escape, BikeRide) -- verified against a suspected early-mechanical variant; none exists, no year split. Unbranded hydros have weak resale. Takeoffs come from riders moving to 4-piston setups.

upgrade.name: 4-piston hydraulic brake set with 203mm rotors
upgrade.note: 73 pounds of bike plus rider at 28mph. Brake like you mean it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Standard saddle economics.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select saddle
upgrade.note: Fat bike, plush ride, might as well finish the job at the seat.

stock_parts.comp_note: Parts-bin value.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Standard fix, standard result.

stock_parts.comp_note: Generic OEM platforms.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Grippy, cheap, done.

bikes.year_notes: T1 family timeline: original T1 high-step and T1 ST launched 2023 (Forbes review 7/6/2023; EBR; Ebike Escape T1/T1ST review) with the Velopower E35+ 350W/45Nm torque-sensor drive and non-removable 36V 353Wh frame pack -- both constant across the run. The T1 ST Plus (2024) revised the electronics: Velotric's own replacement-parts guide assigns the Plus the shared 3.5in color display SKU (D2/S1/T1 ST Plus/N2/B1) while the 2023 originals used a different app-first integrated unit, so the display row is SPLIT by year in v2 -- the only evidenced year-variant component in this dossier. Battery remains excluded from takeoffs (non-removable).
bikes.blurb: 36lb stealth urban bike with a frame-integrated non-removable battery. Battery is NOT a takeoff candidate; the trade-in stream is pure contact points and tires.

stock_parts.comp_note: Sport saddle, personal-fit item, minimal resale.

upgrade.name: Volt saddle
upgrade.note: Fitness posture wants a performance saddle, not a couch. Volt is the safe pick.

stock_parts.comp_note: Parts-bin value.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Light bike, long rides, same palm math as everything else.

stock_parts.comp_note: Generic OEM platforms.

upgrade.name: Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Lighter riders still deserve pedals with pins.

stock_parts.comp_note: OEM slicks with no aftermarket following. Exact stock size unverified in sources, so priced at floor.

upgrade.name: Marathon 700x38 tires (pair)
upgrade.note: A 36lb commuter with flat protection is the whole point of the T1.

stock_parts.stock_part: Velotric integrated display unit (original T1 / T1 ST)
stock_parts.comp_note: YEAR SPLIT (v2): the original 2023 T1/T1 ST used its own frame-integrated display with the Velotric app as the primary interface (Forbes 7/2023; Ebike Escape T1/T1ST review) -- it is NOT the shared 3.5in SKU that Velotric's parts guide reserves for Plus-era models. Crash-replacement demand only; no solds found, priced at floor.

YEAR SPLIT (v2): Velotric's replacement guide groups the T1 ST Plus with the D2/S1/N2/B1 3.5in display SKU -- that applies to 2024+ Plus models only; 2023 originals use the separate integrated unit (see 2023 row). catalog_name kept as the existing shared SKU so it merges with the DB. Factory replacements cap the price; no solds found.
Hydraulic from the factory across the T1 family; takeoffs are crash-repair driven only. No year split evidenced.
